article_meta.title: By Jonah Goldberg July 11, 2016 11:14 AM PT Share via Close extra sharing options Email Facebook X LinkedIn Threads Redd

reasoning: The model correctly focused on the author's own prose and applied mostly appropriate bias labels, but it missed multiple clear instances of biased author language. No attribution violations occurred.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged passages are from the author's own narrative voice; no quoted or attributed source language was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Each flagged item reflects genuine author-level bias (loaded metaphors, evaluative generalizations, pejorative descriptors).
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed several salient biased phrases in author prose, e.g., 'knocked the lazy certainty out of almost everybody,' 'It was a welcome change,' '(who did not lose his lazy certainty),' 'tribe... ideological bunkers,' and 'That's true, and tragic, and fairly irrelevant.'
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels are largely defensible; one could argue 'obtuse' is closer to Mudslinging/Ad Hominem or Subjective Adjectives than Spin, but overall types are reasonable.
